entities.name: Fitbit
entities.context: Default data source for health metrics ingestion (resting HR, HRV, weight)

entities.name: SQLite
entities.context: Database backend used via Python sqlite3 module for storing daily health metrics

entities.name: daily_resting_hr
entities.context: Database table storing resting heart rate, HRV, SpO2, skin temp delta, and stress score

entities.name: daily_weight
entities.context: Database table storing weight in kg, BMI, and body fat percentage

entities.name: Health Biometrics Tracking
entities.context: System for upserting daily health metrics with conflict resolution (upsert pattern)

entities.name: HRV
entities.context: Heart rate variability metric tracked daily alongside resting HR

entities.name: SpO2
entities.context: Blood oxygen saturation tracked as optional daily metric

entities.name: Stress Score
entities.context: Optional daily stress metric sourced from Fitbit

entities.name: BMI
entities.context: Body mass index tracked alongside daily weight

entities.name: Body Fat Percentage
entities.context: Optional fat_pct metric stored with daily weight entries

entities.name: Upsert Pattern
entities.context: INSERT ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE pattern used to idempotently store daily metrics

relationships.context: Fitbit is the default source for resting HR, HRV, SpO2, skin temp, and stress data
Fitbit is the default source for weight, BMI, and body fat data
Table stored in SQLite database via sqlite3 connection
Table stored in SQLite database via sqlite3 connection
All daily metric writes use upsert to handle duplicate dates idempotently
Fitbit serves as the primary data source for the biometrics tracking system
HRV is an optional column in the daily_resting_hr table
SpO2 is an optional column in the daily_resting_hr table
Stress score is an optional column in the daily_resting_hr table
BMI is an optional column in the daily_weight table
fat_pct is an optional column in the daily_weight table
